headline: 30 Beluga Whales Escape Captivity After Years of Abandonment
meta_description: US and Canadian governments approve transfer of 30 beluga whales stranded at closed Ontario theme park to ethical aquarium homes.
summary_short: After years trapped at a shuttered Ontario theme park, 30 intelligent beluga whales have been approved for rescue to proper facilities.
body:
For years, 30 beluga whales languished at Marineland near Niagara Falls, a once-popular theme park that closed as public opposition to marine mammal captivity grew. The facility, stripped of funding and unable to care for the animals, had even threatened euthanasia if relocation permits weren't approved.

Now, after a complex international approval process, the whales have received their lifeline. Both NOAA and Canada's Department of Fisheries and Oceans issued permits allowing 28 belugas to transfer to four American aquariums—Georgia Aquarium, Chicago's Shedd Aquarium, and two SeaWorld facilities in San Diego and San Antonio. Spain's authorities are reviewing permits for the remaining two destined for Oceanogràfic València.

The rescue reflects the whales' exceptional intelligence and emotional needs. One captive male named No-see famously mimicked human speech patterns so accurately that he convinced a diver to exit the tank by imitating safety orders. These remarkable creatures—known as "white whales" for their unique pale skin—require specialized, dignified environments that Marineland simply couldn't provide.

Once Canadian veterinarians clear the animals for travel, aquarium experts will escort them to homes offering proper nutrition, controlled water quality, and medical care. The entire mobilization will take several weeks, but represents a crucial second chance for animals whose intelligence demands nothing less than compassionate treatment.
